name_romaji: Hitachi Kokubun Sōji Ato Shutsudo Kogawara
name_en: Old Roof Tiles Excavated from the Hitachi Provincial Monastery Site
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the archaeological materials category: old roof tiles excavated from the site of the Hitachi provincial monastery. Municipally designated by Ishioka City on August 23, 1978. Located in Sōja 1-chōme.

name_romaji: Hitachi Kokubun Niji Ato Shutsudo Kogawara
name_en: Old Roof Tiles Excavated from the Hitachi Provincial Nunnery Site
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the archaeological materials category: old roof tiles excavated from the site of the Hitachi provincial nunnery. Municipally designated by Ishioka City on August 23, 1978. Located in Sōja 1-chōme.

name_romaji: Hitachi Kokubun Niji Ato Shutsudo Bokusho Doki
name_en: Ink-Inscribed Earthenware Excavated from the Hitachi Provincial Nunnery Site
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the archaeological materials category: ink-inscribed earthenware excavated from the site of the Hitachi provincial nunnery. Municipally designated by Ishioka City on August 23, 1978. Located in Someya.

name_romaji: Funatsukayama Kofungun Jūnanagōfun Shutsudo Tankō, Tate, Chokutō
name_en: Cuirass, Shield, and Straight Sword Excavated from Tumulus No. 17 of the Funatsukayama Tumulus Group
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the archaeological materials category: a cuirass, shield, and straight sword excavated from Tumulus No. 17 of the Funatsukayama tumulus group. Municipally designated by Ishioka City on August 23, 1978. Located in Someya.

name_romaji: Ibaraki Haiji Soseki Ichigō, Nigō, Sangō, Yongō
name_en: Foundation Stones Nos. 1 to 4 of the Ibaraki Temple Ruins
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the archaeological materials category: foundation stones nos. 1 to 4 of the Ibaraki temple ruins. Municipally designated by Ishioka City on June 27, 1980. Located in Kokufu 5-chōme.

name_en: Foundation Stone No. 5 of the Ibaraki Temple Ruins
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the archaeological materials category: foundation stone no. 5 of the Ibaraki temple ruins. Municipally designated by Ishioka City on June 27, 1980. Located in Kaichi 2-chōme.

name_en: Foundation Stone No. 6 of the Ibaraki Temple Ruins
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the archaeological materials category: foundation stone no. 6 of the Ibaraki temple ruins. Municipally designated by Ishioka City on June 27, 1980. Located in Kokufu 6-chōme.

name_en: Foundation Stone No. 7 of the Ibaraki Temple Ruins
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the archaeological materials category: foundation stone no. 7 of the Ibaraki temple ruins. Municipally designated by Ishioka City on November 8, 1985. Located in Kokufu 6-chōme.

name_romaji: Daijō Myōten Nihon Kaikoku Kuyōhi (Nihon Chizu Senkokuiri)
name_en: Memorial Stele for a Nationwide Sutra Pilgrimage, with an Incised Map of Japan
description_en: A tangible cultural property: a memorial stele commemorating a nationwide Buddhist sutra pilgrimage, incised with a map of Japan. Municipally designated by Ishioka City on July 28, 1983. Located in Someya.

name_en: Tomita Sasara Lion-Dance Regalia (Young Lion, Female Lion, Old Lion)
description_en: A tangible folk cultural property: the sasara lion-dance regalia of Tomita, comprising the young lion, female lion, and old lion. Municipally designated by Ishioka City on November 25, 1981. Located in Kokufu.

name_romaji: Someya Jūniza Kagura (Sarutahiko no Men hoka Nijūroku)
name_en: Masks of the Someya Twelve-Act Kagura (Sarutahiko Mask and 26 Others)
description_en: A tangible folk cultural property: masks of the Someya twelve-act kagura, including the Sarutahiko mask and twenty-six others. Municipally designated by Ishioka City on November 25, 1981. Located in Someya.

name_en: Lion-Dance Head of Nakanouchi-chō
description_en: A tangible folk cultural property: a lion-dance head of Nakanouchi-chō. Municipally designated by Ishioka City. Located in Fuchū 1-chōme.

name_romaji: Nakamachi no Dashi Ningyō 'Yamato Takeru' Ittai
name_en: Festival-Float Figure of Yamato Takeru of Nakamachi (One Figure)
description_en: A tangible folk cultural property: a festival-float figure of Yamato Takeru of Nakamachi. Municipally designated by Ishioka City. Located in Kokufu 3-chōme.

name_romaji: Kanamarumachi no Dashi Ningyō 'Benzaiten' Ittai (Tsuketari Kanamarumachi no Dashi Kazari Isshiki)
name_en: Festival-Float Figure of Benzaiten of Kanamaru-chō (One Figure, with a Set of Float Decorations)
description_en: A tangible folk cultural property: a festival-float figure of Benzaiten of Kanamaru-chō, together with a set of float decorations. Municipally designated by Ishioka City. Located in Kokufu 2-chōme.

name_en: Festival Float of Kanamaru-chō
description_en: A tangible folk cultural property: the festival float of Kanamaru-chō. Municipally designated by Ishioka City. Located in Sōja 1-chōme.

name_en: Daidai Kagura Sacred Dance (Twelve-Mask Kagura)
description_en: An intangible folk cultural property: the Daidai kagura sacred dance, also known as the twelve-mask kagura. Municipally designated by Ishioka City on November 26, 1971. Located in Kakioka.

name_en: Negoya Hereditary Twelve-Act Kagura Dance
description_en: An intangible folk cultural property: the hereditary twelve-act kagura dance of Negoya. Municipally designated by Ishioka City on December 22, 1972. Located in Negoya.

name_en: Sasara Dance of Kakioka Arajuku
description_en: An intangible folk cultural property: the sasara dance of Kakioka Arajuku. Municipally designated by Ishioka City. Located in Kakioka.

name_en: Lion Dance of Kakioka Tate
description_en: An intangible folk cultural property: the lion dance of Kakioka Tate. Municipally designated by Ishioka City. Located in Kakioka.

name_en: Giant Doll of Daida (Commonly Called Daidarabotchi)
description_en: An intangible folk cultural property: the giant doll of Daida, commonly called Daidarabotchi. Municipally designated by Ishioka City. Located in Iseki.

name_romaji: Hitachi no Kuni Sōsha-gū Sairei no Shishi, Dashi, Sasara Gyōji
name_en: Lion, Float, and Sasara Events of the Hitachi Sōsha-gū Shrine Festival
description_en: An intangible folk cultural property: the lion, float, and sasara events of the Hitachi Sōsha-gū shrine festival. Municipally designated by Ishioka City on October 20, 2021. Located in Sōja 2-chōme and elsewhere.

name_en: Garden of Daikaku-ji Temple
description_en: A monument designated as a place of scenic beauty: the garden of Daikaku-ji temple. Municipally designated by Ishioka City on March 15, 1968. Located in Ōmasu.

name_en: Narutaki Waterfall
description_en: A monument designated as a place of scenic beauty: the Narutaki waterfall. Municipally designated by Ishioka City. Located in Kawaraya.

name_en: Itohiba Cypress of Sugama
description_en: A monument designated as a natural monument: an itohiba cypress of Sugama. Municipally designated by Ishioka City on November 7, 1972. Located in Sugama.

name_en: Japanese White Pine of Nakayama
description_en: A monument designated as a natural monument: a Japanese white pine of Nakayama. Municipally designated by Ishioka City on November 7, 1972. Located in Obata.

name_en: Habitat of the Himeharuzemi Cicada
description_en: A monument designated as a natural monument: a habitat of the himeharuzemi cicada. Municipally designated by Ishioka City on December 14, 1976. Located in Shōbuzawa.

name_en: Habitat of the Himeharuzemi Cicada
description_en: A monument designated as a natural monument: a habitat of the himeharuzemi cicada. Municipally designated by Ishioka City on December 14, 1976. Located in Oyamada.

name_en: Great Wild Cherry Tree of Kamiaoyagi
description_en: A monument designated as a natural monument: a great wild cherry tree of Kamiaoyagi. Municipally designated by Ishioka City. Located in Kamiaoyagi.

name_en: Yūki Gion Festival Music (Tama)
description_en: An intangible folk cultural property: the Yūki Gion festival music of Tama. Municipally designated by Yūki City on April 24, 1967. Located in Ōaza Tama, Yūki City.

name_en: Yūki Gion Festival Music (Ōki)
description_en: An intangible folk cultural property: the Yūki Gion festival music of Ōki. Municipally designated by Yūki City on September 20, 1967. Located in Ōaza Ōki, Yūki City.

name_en: Yūki Gion Festival Music (Komori)
description_en: An intangible folk cultural property: the Yūki Gion festival music of Komori. Municipally designated by Yūki City on September 20, 1967. Located in Ōaza Komori, Yūki City.

name_en: Yūki Gion Festival Music (Honda and Terauchi)
description_en: An intangible folk cultural property: the Yūki Gion festival music of Honda and Terauchi. Municipally designated by Yūki City on September 20, 1967. Located in Ōaza Odabayashi (Honda and Terauchi), Yūki City.

name_en: Yūki Gion Festival Music (Furushinden and Kuroda)
description_en: An intangible folk cultural property: the Yūki Gion festival music of Furushinden and Kuroda. Municipally designated by Yūki City on September 20, 1967. Located in Ōaza Odabayashi (Furushinden and Kuroda), Yūki City.

name_en: Grave of Yūki Tomomitsu
description_en: A monument designated as a historic site: the grave of Yūki Tomomitsu. Municipally designated by Yūki City on October 1, 1963. Located in Ōaza Yūki (Uramachi), Yūki City.

name_en: Mausoleum of the Yūki Family at Jigen-in
description_en: A monument designated as a historic site: the mausoleum of the Yūki family at Jigen-in. Municipally designated by Yūki City on October 1, 1963. Located in Ōaza Yūki (Kobana), Yūki City.

name_en: Hodozuka Mound
description_en: A monument designated as a historic site: the Hodozuka mound. Municipally designated by Yūki City on October 1, 1963. Located in Ōaza Kanakubo, Yūki City.

name_en: Hayashi Hachiman Mound
description_en: A monument designated as a historic site: the Hayashi Hachiman mound. Municipally designated by Yūki City on October 1, 1963. Located in Ōaza Hayashi, Yūki City.

name_en: Satellite Mound of the Hayashi Hachiman Mound
description_en: A monument designated as a historic site: the satellite mound of the Hayashi Hachiman mound. Municipally designated by Yūki City on October 1, 1963. Located in Ōaza Hayashi, Yūki City.

name_en: Bicchūzuka Mound
description_en: A monument designated as a historic site: the Bicchūzuka mound. Municipally designated by Yūki City on October 1, 1963. Located in Ōaza Kamiyamakawa, Yūki City.

name_en: Furuyama Hachiman Mound
description_en: A monument designated as a historic site: the Furuyama Hachiman mound. Municipally designated by Yūki City on October 1, 1963. Located in Ōaza Kamiyamakawa, Yūki City.
